description: The nine offices of the Bay. Spoken in Ealdforn, written in Latin, understood in modern English. Each office has an activation condition, succession rules, and duties.

offices.duty: Presides over the Assembly. First among equals. In later eras, may be elected Imperator with temporary singular authority.
Carries the harvest. Manages the grain tithe, the common stores, and the treasury. Audits public accounts.
offices.succession: Appointed by the Assembly; Grange-hall traditionally nominates

offices.duty: Counts the people. Registers ships, births, deaths, and property. Conducts the census every five years. Knows what the Bay actually is.
Deems disputes. Freezes conflicts when the Assembly cannot resolve them. Guards the judgment until it is enforced. Serves on the Codex Court when established.
offices.succession: Nominated by Imperator, confirmed by Elders (post-Imperator reform)

offices.duty: Appointed only in crisis. Commands the harbor watch and all military forces. Absolute authority for a fixed term. Must step down when the crisis ends. The Cantica is spoken at their appointment: 'Res publica in periculo est.'
offices.term: Duration of crisis. Maximum one year unless renewed by Assembly.
offices.succession: Appointed by the Assembly in crisis session

offices.duty: Maintains the calendar. Announces solstices, equinoxes, feast days, election cycles, and watch rotations. Controls the dates — and dates control everything.
offices.seat: The sunrise room in the Assembly hall
offices.succession: Appointed by the Assembly; traditionally a neutral figure

offices.duty: Inspects and maintains public works: aqueducts, granaries, watchtowers, harbor pilings, market squares. Knows every loose stone in the Bay.
offices.seat: No fixed hall. The Weard walks the walls.

offices.duty: Writes the laws. Copies the records. Maintains the Scriptorium archives. The most numerous office in the Bay — hundreds by Year 100.
offices.term: Indefinite. Serve at pleasure of the Sceawere.
offices.succession: Trained through apprenticeship in the Scriptorium

offices.duty: Announces the Assembly's resolutions in the public square. The voice of the law before it is read. Speaks the Cantica at public ceremonies.
offices.seat: The harbor square. No fixed hall.
offices.succession: Appointed by the Assembly as needed
